description: Join us for a sensory exploration on the farm! Through hands-on activities, students will learn how we use our five senses to experience the world around us. Then, we'll meet some of our friendly farm animals.

metadata.title: Exploring Senses on the Farm | Ardenwood Historic Farm | East Bay Regional Park District

description: Discover what life was like for kids on a farm through hands-on activities. Students will learn about the daily chores children would help with, from preparing animal food to feeding the farm animals.

metadata.title: Farmyard Chores for Kids | Ardenwood Historic Farm | East Bay Regional Park District

description: Step back in time and explore life on a turn-of-the-last-century farm. Students will watch history come to life through hands-on, seasonal activities utilizing historic tools. Activities could include getting your hands dirty while working the land, learning about old-time farming practices, and helping feed our farm animals.

description:
Meet our sheep and discover how their wool is transformed into the clothes we wear! This hands-on program explores the wool processing journey—from shearing and carding to spinning and knitting. Students will compare both historical and modern methods of wool processing, seeing how the craft has evolved over time. 
This program is perfect for winter and can be comfortably hosted in one of our indoor farmyard spaces in any weather conditions.

metadata.title: Wonderful Wool  | Ardenwood Historic Farm | East Bay Regional Park District
metadata.description:

This program is perfect for winter and can be comfortably hosted in one of our indoor farmyard spaces in any weather conditions.

description: Explore the important role pollinators play in producing our food. Through engaging activities and hands-on investigations, students will learn about the process of pollination and what we can do to support our pollinators.

limited_availability: Only available April through mid-November on select Thursdays
description: Our Farmyard Station Day features a 30-minute, Naturalist-led program and interactive, self-guided stations throughout the park. Stations may consist of grinding & shelling corn, carding wool, games on the lawn, Patterson house tours, train rides, old-fashioned laundry, and visiting the farm animals. Naturalist program times and other scheduled activities (house tours and train times) are assigned based on availability. Please note station days are only available on certain days each month.

description: Explore the journey of food from the field to the dinner plate as a Naturalist guides your students through an 1890s historic farm. Compare and contrast historical farming and cooking methods with modern technology as we transform crops into food.

description: Trace the evolution of farming in California with a virtual tour of Ardenwood. Students will explore historic buildings and artifacts, as well as maps, photographs, diaries, and other primary sources, to discover the people and events that helped shape their community.

metadata.title: California's Farming History | Ardenwood Historic Farm | East Bay Regional Park District
